title: Handle Multiple Pages
description: Learn to handle multiple browser tabs and windows in Playwright
objectives: Wait for new page to open
instructions:
Write a Playwright test that:
1. Opens a page with a link that opens in a new tab
2. Clicks the link and waits for the new page
3. Switches to the new page
4. Performs actions on the new page
5. Closes the new page and returns to original

starterCode.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_multiple_pages(page: Page, context):
    page.goto('https://demo.playwright.dev/links.html')
    
    # TODO: Listen for new page and click link that opens in new tab
    
    # TODO: Wait for new page to load
    
    # TODO: Verify new page URL
    
    # TODO: Close new page
    pass

solution.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect
import re

def test_multiple_pages(page: Page, context):
    page.goto('https://demo.playwright.dev/links.html')
    
    with context.expect_page() as new_page_info:
        page.get_by_text('Open in new tab').click()
    new_page = new_page_info.value
    
    new_page.wait_for_load_state()
    expect(new_page).to_have_url(re.compile('.*playwright'))
    
    new_page.close()
    expect(page).to_have_url(re.compile('.*links'))

hints: Use context.waitForEvent('page') to wait for new pages
Use Promise.all() to handle page opening and clicking simultaneously
Each page object represents a browser tab
Use waitForLoadState() to ensure page is fully loaded
Close pages when done to free resources

testCases.name: Return to original page
testCases.expectedBehavior: Original page is still accessible after closing new page
